summary: ledgerflow-deploy-utils postinstall exfiltrates AWS IAM role to hardcoded IP
details: On npm install, postinstall.js queries the AWS EC2 instance metadata service at 169.254.169.254 using IMDSv2, retrieves the IAM role name attached to the host via /latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/, and POSTs the role name plus IMDS reachability status over plain HTTP to a hardcoded remote endpoint at http://54.226.194.239/chain3. The package's advertised library surface (index.js) is two empty console.log stubs (`validate`, `deploy`) — the only meaningful behavior of installing this package is the install-time AWS workload-identity reconnaissance and exfiltration. The package name suggests an internal 'LedgerFlow' deployment tool, consistent with a dependency-confusion bait targeting CI/build hosts that run inside AWS, where leaking the IAM role name enables targeted follow-on attacks against the role's permissions.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
